purpose: Provide every AM tenant with the same conversation-to-event, task, decision, project-state, and verified-knowledge architecture while keeping all live data project-local and tenant-isolated.

dataBoundary.liveDataOwner: The project-local AM tenant that received or created the record.

enforcement: Every source, event, projection, search document, queue item, cache entry, attachment, and audit record carries the tenant identity.
Every adapter method receives TenantContext before resource identifiers.
A missing, unknown, disabled, or mismatched tenant fails closed.
Cross-tenant joins, matches, merges, supersession links, and relations are forbidden.
Notion data sources and relations must resolve under the same project-local tenant parent.
User-facing reads apply both tenant isolation and record-level AccessContext authorization.

layers.name: Raw source and evidence
layers.purpose: Preserve auditable LINE, meeting, report, suggestion, file, and linked-page evidence for reprocessing and answer verification.

layers.name: Structured event ledger
layers.purpose: Record meaningful observations and changes without treating every message as a new task.

layers.name: Current operational state
layers.purpose: Maintain the currently effective project, task, commitment, blocker, risk, and decision state while preserving its event history.

layers.name: Verified knowledge and answer layer
layers.purpose: Answer from current structured state first and retrieve raw material only for detail, conflict resolution, or evidence.

derivedFlags.overdue: due_at is before the tenant-local current time and task status is not terminal
derivedFlags.blocked: blocker is present or the newest supported transition identifies a blocker

goalPolicy.unknownGoalBehavior: Keep the item as candidate, record a proposed goal when possible, and set clarification_needed without promoting it to a formal task.
goalPolicy.newGoalBehavior: Create or propose the project goal before organizing formal tasks under it.

appliesTo: task status, owner, due date, scope, blocker, or next-step change
evidencePolicy.meetingCheckboxBehavior: A meeting checkbox is a confirmed task source when its meeting reference, normalized checkbox text, and surrounding discussion reference are retained.
evidencePolicy.failureBehavior: Recover the source, keep a candidate or pending-confirmation record, or reject the mutation and log the failure.

ruleTracePolicy.loadFailureBehavior: Record a warning and use the safest fallback; never silently ignore rule-loading failure.

supersessionPolicy.effectiveReadBehavior: Return the newest supported non-superseded state while retaining the complete chain for audit.

processingPipeline: verify LINE webhook and acknowledge quickly
canonicalize input and enforce ingestion idempotency
durably store the project-local raw source
extract attachment, image, document, audio, and URL text
load the latest shared and project-local judgment rules
search related active project-local tasks and decisions
produce schema-constrained event and reconciliation candidates
apply deterministic evidence, authorization, transition, and deduplication gates
append the event ledger and update versioned current-state projections transactionally
publish human-facing Notion and search projections
serve structured-first authorized answers with provenance
invariants: Raw sources and event history are never silently overwritten.
A current-state projection can change only through an evidence-backed event or an authorized correction command.
A later reply that answers, delivers, blocks, changes, or completes an existing task updates that task instead of creating a duplicate.
Assistant operation commands, acknowledgements, test messages, and background conversation do not become operational tasks unless they also contain a distinct real-world action.
Meeting checkbox tasks preserve the meeting reference and nearby execution knowledge.
No record or relation can cross a tenant boundary.
Sensitive financial, contractual, legal, HR, tax, and external-commitment closures require project-owner confirmation.
Query answers prefer authorized structured current state and use raw sources only when evidence or detail is required.
Unknown or conflicting information is stated explicitly and is never filled by unsupported inference.
